title: Arithmetic operation

content.problemStatement:
Write a C++ program that takes two integers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input.

Use a 'switch' statement to perform the corresponding arithmetic operation and print the result.

Check the sample input / output below for clarity.

(**Note:** It is guaranteed that in the hidden test cases, the second number will not be 0 during division.)
constraints: op is a character; only '+', '-', '*', '/' are considered valid
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers num1 and num2, and a character op, separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the result of the arithmetic operation, or 'Invalid operator' if op is not one of '+', '-', '*', '/'.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. 5+2=7, 10/10=1 (integer division), 7*3=21.
The operator '%' is not among the allowed symbols, so the program prints the error message.
Integer division 9/2 truncates toward zero, yielding 4.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the two integers and the operator. Use a switch statement on the operator to compute the required arithmetic operation. If the operator is not one of the four allowed symbols, output the error message "Invalid operator". All operations are integer operations, and division uses integer truncation. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input and output buffers.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i is the test case index (1-based). Generate the input string for hidden test case i.
    # Use RINT(a,b) for random integers in [a,b] and POWER(a,b) for a**b.
    if i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1 +')
    elif i == 2:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1 -')
    elif i == 3:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1 /')
    elif i == 4:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1 %')
    elif i == 5:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('100 100 +')
    elif i == 6:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('100 100 *')
    elif i == 7:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('100 99 /')
    elif i == 8:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('100 100 ^')
    elif i == 9:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        for _ in range(t):
            print('5 5 +')
    elif i == 10:
        t = 3
        print(t)
        for _ in range(t):
            print('10 5 -')
    elif i == 11:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        print('1 2 +')
        print('2 3 -')
        print('3 4 *')
        print('4 5 /')
    elif i == 12:
        t = 4
        print(t)
        print('5 4 +')
        print('4 3 -')
        print('3 2 *')
        print('2 1 /')
    elif i == 13:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('99 99 *')
    elif i == 14:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('97 89 +')
    elif i == 15:
        t = 2
        print(t)
        print('50 25 /')
        print('25 50 -')
    elif i == 16:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('2 3 /')
    elif i == 17:
        t = 5
        print(t)
        print('10 5 +')
        print('20 4 -')
        print('3 7 *')
        print('8 2 /')
        print('6 6 %')
    elif i == 18:
        t = 6
        print(t)
        print('1 2 +')
        print('2 3 -')
        print('3 4 *')
        print('4 5 /')
        print('5 6 +')
        print('6 7 -')
    elif i == 19:
        t = 10
        print(t)
        print('9 1 +')
        print('8 2 -')
        print('7 3 *')
        print('6 4 /')
        print('5 5 %')
        print('4 6 +')
        print('3 7 -')
        print('2 8 *')
        print('1 9 /')
        print('10 10 ^')
    elif i == 20:
        t = 10
        print(t)
        print('100 1 +')
        print('99 2 -')
        print('98 3 *')
        print('97 4 /')
        print('96 5 %')
        print('95 6 +')
        print('94 7 -')
        print('93 8 *')
        print('92 9 /')
        print('91 10 ^')
    else:
        # default fallback
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1 +')
